data.title: BlackRock's Long Position In H Shares Of ZTE Corp Increases To 6.31% - HKEX
data.description:
HKEX:

  • BLACKROCK'S LONG POSITION IN H SHARES OF ZTE CORP INCREASES TO 6.31% ON JUNE 27 FROM 5.76%

data.title: Eurozone Current Account Surplus Widens in Q1
data.description:
The euro area recorded a current account surplus of 366 billion euros in the first quarter of 2025, up from 319 billion euros in the prior-year period, data from the European Central Bank showed Thursday.

The reading reflects larger surpluses for goods and services, a lower surplus for primary income, and a growing secondary income deficit.

data.title: Bank Of America Corp's Long Position In H Shares Of Dongfeng Motor Decreases To 5.54% - HKEX
data.description:
HKEX:

  • BANK OF AMERICA CORP'S LONG POSITION IN H SHARES OF DONGFENG MOTOR DECREASES TO 5.54% ON JUNE 30 FROM 6.05%

  • BANK OF AMERICA CORP'S SHORT POSITION IN H SHARES OF DONGFENG MOTOR DECREASES TO 5.39% ON JUNE 30 FROM 5.89%

data.title: World Co. Retail Sales Down by 2% in June
data.description:
World Co domestic retail sales decreased by 2.2% in June, according to a Thursday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

Store sales fell 2.5%, while e-commerce sales slipped 1.2%, said the apparel company. Same-store sales declined by 3.5%.

The retailer decreased its retail stores by two to 2,023 in June.

data.title: Asian Star Anchor Chain's Unit Subscribes to Wealth Management Product for 30 Million Yuan
data.description:
Asian Star Anchor Chain's unit, Asian Star (Zhenjiang) Mooring Chain, subscribed to a broker wealth management product for 30.1 million yuan, according to a Shanghai bourse filing on Thursday.

The product, Xin Ying Stable (Beginning of Quarter) No. 3 FOF Plan, will have a term of 150 days.

The Chinese anchor chain manufacturer subscribed to the product from CITIC Securities Asset Management.

Asian Star's shares closed over 4% lower.

data.title: Volvo Cars Signs Contract to Produce Polestar 7 Electric SUVs in Slovakia
data.description:
Volvo Car AB (VOLCAR-B.ST), d/b/a Volvo Cars, signed a nonbinding agreement to develop and produce Swedish automotive manufacturer Polestar's new electric compact SUV, Polestar 7, at its plant in Kosice, Slovakia.

The Swedish automaker said Thursday that the new model is expected to be launched in 2028 and will be the second vehicle to be built at the facility, subject to final agreement.

Meanwhile, Polestar 7 will share its technology base with future Volvo models, benefiting from component sharing, next-gen battery tech, and over-the-air software upgrades.

data.title: Singapore's Straits Times Index Closes at Record-High — Market Talk
data.description: Singapore's FTSE Straits Times Index closed 0.2% higher at 4019.57, hitting a record-high. The benchmark index crossed the 4000 mark for the first time in March. Maybank Research analysts expect capital rotations to accelerate amid uncertainties regarding U.S. trade policies. "Singapore stands to benefit given strong policy certainty, and a government with a clear mandate to cushion extreme outcomes," they said in a recent note. Among advancers, DFI Retail Group rose 5.0%, City Developments climbed 2.2% and Hongkong Land was up 1.7%. Meanwhile, UOL Group fell 1.2% and Keppel DC REIT lost 0.85%. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: REG - Keystone Law Grp PLC - Result of AGM
data.description:
RNS Number : 6284P Keystone Law Group PLC 03 July 2025  

3 July 2025

Keystone Law Group Plc

('Keystone Law', the 'Group' or the 'Company')

Result of Annual General Meeting

Keystone Law Group plc (AIM: KEYS), the premier tech-enabled platform law firm, announces that at the Company's Annual General Meeting held on 3 July 2025, all resolutions put to shareholders were passed unanimously on a show of hands.

The proxy figures for the resolutions will be made available on the Company's website later today.

For further information please contact:

Keystone Law Group plc

James Knight, Chief Executive Officer

Ashley Miller, Finance Director

www.keystonelaw.com

 +44 (0) 20 3319 3700

Panmure Liberum Limited (Nominated Adviser and Joint Broker)

Atholl Tweedie (Corporate Finance)

Rupert Dearden (Corporate Broking)

www.panmureliberum.com

+44 (0) 20 7886 2500

Investec Bank plc (Joint Broker)

Carlton Nelson

James Rudd

www.investec.co.uk

+44 (0) 20 7597 5970

Vigo Consulting (Financial Public Relations)                                               

Jeremy Garcia / Fiona Hetherington

keystonelaw@vigoconsulting.com

+44 (0) 20 7390 0233

Notes to editors

Keystone (AIM: KEYS) is a highly scalable, premier tech-enabled platform law firm. Ranked within the UK Top 100 law firms, providing conventional legal services in a £12bn addressable market through its differentiated platform model which has three defining characteristics:

·      Lawyers have freedom, flexibility and autonomy, and are paid up to 75% of what they bill.

·      Lawyers determine how, when and where they work, in contrast to the conventional law firm model.

·      Lawyers are provided full infrastructure and support via its central office team, bespoke user-friendly IT platform, and network of colleagues and events.

Keystone is a full-service law firm, with 20 service areas and more than 50 industry sectors delivered by over 450 high calibre self-employed Principal lawyers who work from their own offices.

More information about Keystone can be found at www.keystonelaw.co.uk.

data.title: Auto, pharma stocks rally up to 6% amid US-India trade deal optimism
data.description:
The shares of export-oriented auto and pharma companies recorded significant gains on July 3 as markets remained optimistic around the possibility of India-US trade deal being announced soon.

The sharp rise in the auto stocks pushed the Nifty Auto index up 0.75 percent in the afternoon to hover around 24,065, extending gains for the third consecutive session. The Nifty Pharma index meanwhile rose 0.35 percent to stand at 22,188, extending gains for the 10th consecutive session.

India and US are on the verge of a breakthrough in their negotiations for a mini-trade deal, and an announcement could come as early as this weekend, people familiar with the matter told Moneycontrol.

"Last night, talks reached a juncture where a breakthrough is possible, therefore it is not an impossibility that the deal may be announced sometime this week," one of the sources said. India has stuck to its stance on sensitive sectors such as agriculture and dairy.

US President Donald Trump recently said that the trade deal with India will involve "much less tariffs". "I think we are going to have a deal with India. And that is going to be a different kind of a deal. It is going to be a deal where we are able to go in and compete,” Trump said. “Right now, India does not accept anybody in. I think India is going to do that, and if they do that, we are going to have a deal for much less tariffs,” he added.

US recently struck a trade deal with Vietnam. Trump said that imports from Vietnam will now face 20 percent tariff, with some facing even lower rates. The capped 20 percent rate may have further boosted hopes for a positive outcome of US' trade deal with India.

Bosch shares were the top gainer among the auto stocks, jumping nearly 6 percent to trade at Rs 34,390 apiece. Hero MotoCorp and Maruti Suzuki shares gained over 1 percent each. Samvardhana Motherson and Mahindra & Mahindra (M&M) shares gained nearly 1 percent, while those of Tata Motors, Bajaj Auto, Exide Industries and MRF shares were trading in the green with marginal gains.

Natco Pharma shares were the top gainer on the pharma index, rallying over 5 percent to trade at Rs 969 apiece. Ajanta Pharma shares gained over 3.5 percent, while IPCA Labs shares rose nearly 3 percent. Biocon and Dr Reddy’s Laboratories shares were up nearly 2 percent. Cipla, Glenmark Pharma, Zydus Life, Sun Pharma and other pharma stocks were trading in the green with marginal gains.

US President Donald Trump had unveiled a slew of reciprocal tariffs on a number of countries in April this year, triggering a global trade war. He however later announced a 90-day pause to majority of the tariffs, which is set to expire this month.

Trump had said that nations must arrive at a deal with US in order to escape his tariffs which were set to take effect this July. India and the US have been negotiating a Bilateral Trade Agreement (BTA) before the critical July 9 deadline of the 90-day pause on tariff escalations lapses.Also read: Our LIVE blog on stock market updates

data.title: Rockfire Resources Conditionally Raises GBP2 Million
data.description:
Rockfire Resources conditionally raised 2 million pounds sterling in gross proceeds through a share placement.

Acam led the placing and subscribed for 1 million pounds, taking up 16.31% of the total voting rights in the mineral exploration company, according to a Thursday filing. Participants in the placing will receive one warrant for every two ordinary shares subscribed.

Rockfire Resources intends to use net proceeds alongside existing cash to develop its Molaoi zinc/silver/lead project in Greece and for working capital requirements.

data.title: Kelington, PETRONAS CCS to Jointly Study Carbon Capture Opportunities
data.description:
Kelington Group unit Ace Gases Marketing signed a memorandum of understanding with PETRONAS CCS Solutions to jointly study carbon capture opportunities.

The study will assess the feasibility and commercial viability of carbon dioxide logistics and facilities using Ace Gases' expertise, according to a Thursday Malaysian bourse filing.

The MoU will be valid for one year, and can be extended by another year by mutual agreement.

The move supports Malaysia's net-zero target by 2050, with the country's carbon dioxide emissions reaching nearly 289 million tonnes in 2023, it said.

data.description:
** Shares of Johnson Controls-Hitachi Air Conditioning India up 3% to 1,796 rupees

** Co declares interim dividend of 36 rupees per share for FY 2025-26

** Dividend to be paid on or before July 24

** Stock set to rise most in a session since May 12

** More than 100,000 shares traded, 2.2x their 30-day moving average

** JCHA up ~1% YTD

data.title: Japan Service-Sector Sustains Modest Expansion in June: PMI Report
data.description:
Strong new orders and rising employment defined an expansion of Japan's service sector in June, reported S&P Global on Thursday.

Japan's final service purchasing managers index (PMI) rose to 51.7 in June from 51.0 in May, striking further above the 50-mark that separates growth from contraction, said S&P Global citing its monthly survey.

The Japanese service sectors covered include consumer (excluding retail), transport, information, communication, finance, insurance, real estate and business services.

"Panelists indicated that improved customer numbers, marketing activities and firmer demand conditions had helped to lift activity and sales during June," reported S&P Global.

Service sector managers added to payrolls, due in part to rising backlogs but also rising optimism.

"Companies continued to add to their payrolls amid stronger confidence around the 12-month outlook for activity. The level of positive sentiment was the strongest in four months and above the series average," explained S&P Global.

Japan's service providers faced inflation in costs in June, and also raised prices to customers.

"Turning to prices, average input costs continued to rise sharply, despite the rate of inflation dipping to a six-month low. Efforts to pass on expenses to clients drove another increase in output charges, and one that was the most pronounced in 14 months, noted S&P Global.

The Japan Services PMI was compiled by S&P Global from surveys sent to 400 service sector companies from June 12 through June 25.

The Japan composite PMI, a combination of the services and manufacturing sectors, rose to 51.1 in June from 50.2 in May, signaling a third successive

monthly rise in overall business activity across Japan, added S&P Global.
